item.title: U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 2, leaves 6 survivors
item.description: The U.S. military has conducted another strike against a boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing two and leaving six survivors, SOUTHCOM said.

item.title: U.S., Iranian negotiators meet; Trump threatens Iran over Hezbollah
item.description: Their appearance was a rare face-to-face meeting between U.S. and Iranian officials as they launched a 60-day sprint to negotiate over the fate of Iran's nuclear program.

item.title: Trump-backed populist wins tight Colombia presidential vote, sparking protests
item.description: A flamboyant Trump-backed lawyer who's never held public office narrowly won Colombia's polarizing presidential runoff, swinging the country hard right and sparking violent protests.

item.title: LAUSD Superintendent Alberto Carvalho resigns amid FBI investigation
item.description: Nearly four months after federal agents raided his home and district headquarters, Superintendent Alberto Carvalho announced his resignation as the head of the Los Angeles Unified School District on Sunday.

item.title: "Incredible headway" made in Boyle Heights warehouse blaze, LA fire chief says, with smoke expected for 3 more days
item.description: Fire officials said in a news release that as crews continue firefighting operations throughout the day, the volume and color of visible smoke may fluctuate.

item.title: How the World Cup ball has changed over 96 years
item.description: The World Cup has been held for 96 years. The sport of soccer has changed a lot over the last century, and so has the ball. Michael George reports.

item.title: New recommendations to prevent child drownings from American Academy of Pediatrics
item.description: Drowning remains the number one cause of death for children ages 1-4 in the United States. Now, the American Academy of Pediatrics is updating its recommendations on drowning prevention. Jarred Hill reports.

item.title: Americans taking up the art of Ikebana, Japanese flower arrangement
item.description: A hobby celebrating nature that dates back hundreds of years is finding new life in the United States. Michael George reports on why more and more people are giving Japanese flower arranging a try.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Man falls to death during Goose concert at MSG, NYPD says
item.description: A concertgoer fell to his death during rock band Goose's performance Saturday at Madison Square Garden, the New York Police Department said.

item.title: 6/21/2026: Youngest Survivors; What Happened to the Great White Sharks?
item.description: First, a report on the miracle babies of the Mauthausen camp. And, South Africa's missing great white sharks.

item.title: Right-wing candidate holds slim margin in Colombian presidential election
item.description: Colombia faces a tight runoff election between a progressive and a conservative outsider.

item.title: 6/21: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Trump threatens new attacks as U.S. and Iranian negotiators meet; at least 70 major fires burning in western U.S.

item.title: Pilot reports passenger bit fellow flyer on plane approaching Philadelphia
item.description: An American Airlines passenger allegedly bit a fellow flyer and was "trying to fight everybody" on a Sunday flight, a pilot said.

item.title: Rikers Island inmates get Father's Day trip to museum with their kids
item.description: Bradley Blackburn reports on a one-of-a-kind partnership, reuniting incarcerated fathers with their children.

item.title: Ebola outbreak worsening in Congo, response hampered by Trump's USAID cuts
item.description: Ebola is accelerating in the Democratic Republic of the Congo with no approved medicine or vaccine for the deadly virus. Ramy Inocencio has more.

item.title: World Cup fans taking in American experiences like ranch and tipping
item.description: For many international fans in the U.S. for the World Cup, it's their first all-American experience, going beyond the stadiums to visit parks, monuments and celebrating our tastes, like the southern staple Waffle House. Lilia Luciano reports.

item.title: Pilot reports biting passenger on American Airlines flight
item.description: The crew of an American Airlines flight from Charlotte to Philadelphia reported having to deal with a disruptive passenger biting other flyers Sunday. The airline said the person was experiencing a medical emergency.

item.title: At least 70 major fires burning in western U.S.
item.description: At least 70 major fires are burning in several states, fueled by extreme heat, and gusty winds. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Trump says several people arrested for alleged vandalism of Reflecting Pool
item.description: President Trump claims the problems with the Reflecting Pool in Washington are due to vandalism.

item.title: Trump says vandals sabotaged Reflecting Pool, Olympian arrested
item.description: President Trump is accusing vandals of sabotaging the $14 million renovation on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. So far, five people have been arrested, including three-time olympian and cyclist David Hearn, who was taken into custody after touching the pool's detached liner.

item.title: Trump threatens new attacks as U.S. and Iranian negotiators meet
item.description: President Trump is threatening further attacks on Iran, even as Vice President JD Vance, in Switzerland, hails progress at talks aimed at securing peace. Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Wyndham Clark holds on to win U.S. Open
item.description: Wyndham Clark began the final round up six shots, but ended up winning by just one, securing his second U.S. Open title in four years.

item.title: Serena Williams will return to singles tennis at Wimbledon, as a wild card
item.description: Serena Williams recently returned to competition​ in doubles after nearly four years away from professional tennis.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham: "Let's try a diplomatic solution. I think it's going to fail."
item.description: GOP Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina said he expects a diplomatic solution with Iran to fail, though he noted that he would "rather try diplomacy than take it off the table."

item.title: Historic D.C. castle reopens after extensive renovation
item.description: The historic Smithsonian Castle in Washington, D.C., which closed for a multi-year renovation, has temporarily reopened to the public for America's 250th anniversary. Natalie Brand has more.

item.title: Democratic socialism in the midst of a U.S. revival
item.description: Democratic socialism appears to be on the rise in the U.S. The progressive ideology gained prominence when New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani took office. Now, more candidates seem to be gaining momentum in major U.S. cities. Nikole Killion reports.

item.title: Trump defends beautification push in D.C.
item.description: President Trump's push to renovate landmarks in Washington, D.C., ahead of America's 250th birthday is drawing criticism from critics who say the process is being conducted without the usual approvals. Taurean Small explains.

item.title: Despite dip in gas prices, many still feeling economic pinch going into summer travel season
item.description: U.S. drivers are feeling less pain at the pump than in recent months. But for many, it's tough to feel a change. Max Darrow has more.

item.title: Vance heads to Switzerland for Iran peace talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance departed for Switzerland Saturday to begin direct long-term peace negotiations with Iran. His trip comes as shipping in the Strait of Hormuz faced new disruptions after Iran said it was closing the waterway. Imtiaz Tyab reports.
